@attributes.sheet: Positioning - HubSpot- RevOps As a Service

Item: Mid-to-large B2B firms (€5M+), multi-market teams. Priority: Poland; opportunistic: Poland, DACH, Nordics, UK. HubSpot is in place or planned.

Primary: Marketing Leader (Head of Marketing, CMO, Marketing Manager). Secondary: CRO, Sales Leader (Head of Sales, Sales Manager), Head of CS/CS Ops, CEO (Series A companies, companies with maximum 250 employees)
Revenue Operations as a Service (flagship), HubSpot Onboarding, Custom Integrations
Standardize revenue operations on HubSpot: unify tools/data, instrument reliable forecasting & attribution, stand up CS health, and run it with SLAs/weekly cadence

Alternative.ProblemSummary: Disconnected systems, manual admin, no trusted forecasts; leaders fly blind
Alternative.DifferentiationSummary: Assessment -> Backbone -> Run: Operational Reality Assessment, HubSpot process/data standardization, then ongoing SLA-based ops

Alternative.ProblemSummary: Value erodes post go-live; no governance, dashboards decay, adoption slips
Alternative.DifferentiationSummary: RevOps as a Service with weekly status, backlog execution, QA, renewal roadmap—keeps improving each sprint

Alternative.ProblemSummary: Siloed wins; attribution/intent/CS tools don't translate into processes reps and CSMs actually follow
Alternative.DifferentiationSummary: Process-first, tool-second: map data model & playbooks, then wire tools so signals drive action in HubSpot

Solution.WhyThatSucks: Fragmented systems, manual work, no single source of truth; forecasts not believed
Solution.WhatWeDo: Operational Reality Assessment, HubSpot backbone for process, data model, automations; then enablement
Solution.Features: Portal audit, process diagrams, SOPs, roadmap/SOW
Solution.Benefits: Faster time-to-value, shared truth, de-risked scope

Solution.WhyThatSucks: No visibility into activities; can't lift underperformers; forecasting = guesswork
Solution.WhatWeDo: Methodology in HubSpot (pipelines, playbooks, activity/forecast dashboards) + coaching views
Solution.HowWeDoDifferently: Sales methodology operationalization (e.g. SPIN, MEDDPICC, Sandler)
Solution.Capabilities: Sales methodology operationalization (e.g. SPIN, MEDDPICC, Sandler)
Solution.Features: Pipelines, playbooks, activity capture, forecast dashboards
Solution.Benefits: Predictable pipeline, rep lift, forecast accuracy

Solution.WhyThatSucks: Can't prove ROI; lead quality friction with sales
Solution.WhatWeDo: Attribution-first reporting + lifecycle/routing & nurture that sales accepts
Solution.Features: Campaign source tracking, MTA (Multi-Touch Attribution) dashboards, qualification & routing
Solution.Benefits: Credible ROI, fewer rejected leads, smarter budget allocation

Solution.WhyThatSucks: Unknown churn risk, manual tickets, no enablement; CS impact invisible
Solution.WhatWeDo: Health score + ticket integration, playbooks, CS enablement, real-time alerts
Solution.Features: Scoring model, ticket automations, CS dashboards, playbooks
Solution.Benefits: Early churn flags, higher NRR, consistent CS delivery

Solution.WhyThatSucks: Bad data -> broken routing, wasted time; tool chaos
Solution.WhatWeDo: Pre-ingest validation & enrichment, dedup, scheduled hygiene
Solution.Features: Validation rules, dedup logic, enrichment APIs
Solution.Benefits: Clean segments, reliable workflows, better reporting

Solution.WhyThatSucks: Entropy post-launch; no owner; deployments delay, new features not implemented
Solution.WhatWeDo: RevOps as a Service: weekly status, backlog, naming convention hygiene, renewals roadmap, NPS/reviews
Solution.Features: Weekly status, SLAs, hour rules, SOW renewals

Solution.WhyThatSucks: Sync failures & siloed data block GTM plays
Solution.WhatWeDo: Custom implementations architected to your model; treated separately when complex
Solution.Features: Custom objects, APIs, custom apps, custom AI agents
Solution.Benefits: Signals flow to HubSpot; fewer manual fixes
